text.title: Agentic Workflow for AC Authoring and Verification
text.summary: Three-stage chain for generating, verifying, and maintaining acceptance criteria within the SDD lifecycle.

section.title: Three-Stage Chain

rule.statement: Stage 1 (Research): a read-only subagent loads spec.md, design.md, and the data model. It identifies entities, their states, valid transitions, and applicable personas. This output grounds Stage 2 and prevents hallucinated preconditions.
rule.rationale: AC authoring without grounding invents fields and subscription tiers not present in the codebase. The research stage constrains the authoring agent to verifiable reality.

rule.statement: Stage 2 (Authoring): the authoring agent produces Gherkin or rule-based AC in JSON format ({id, title, given[], when[], then[], category}), continuing from the existing ID set. No implementation terms, no And chains longer than 3 in Given, negative cases at least 30% of total.
rule.rationale: Structured JSON output enables downstream tools (ac-coverage.sh, test generators) to parse and process AC without natural-language parsing.

rule.statement: Stage 3 (Verification): the verification agent maps each AC-ID in spec.md to a test reference in test-plan.md and returns {ac_id, has_test, test_ref|null, gap_reason|null}. If any has_test=false, halt and return FAIL. No task moves from in-progress to done with uncovered AC.
rule.rationale: The SDD lifecycle requires test coverage for every AC before a task is considered done. Automated verification prevents the common pattern of shipping with uncovered criteria.

section.reference: Shell script that extracts AC-IDs from spec.md and verifies each has a matching reference in test-plan.md.
Structured XML prompt for Stage 2 authoring with grounding constraints and category requirements.
Structured XML prompt for Stage 3 coverage gate: maps AC-IDs to test references, halts on gaps.

rule.statement: Stakeholder sign-off on AC must happen BEFORE coding starts. Never let the executor agent autonomously generate and execute AC in one chain. Separate: agent drafts → human reviews → agent executes.
rule.rationale: AC are the contract between business and engineering. Auto-executing against unreviewed AC means the wrong contract is implemented — often undiscovered until acceptance testing fails.

rule.statement: Generate AC documentation from .feature files and publish to the SDD done/ snapshot on merge. AC in the done/ snapshot must match production behaviour or CI fails on next run.
rule.rationale: AC that drift from production behaviour after code changes produce false confidence in the test suite. Living docs generated from source prevent divergence.
